data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Netflix follows a microservices architecture allowing for modular scaling and continuous delivery.
data.technical_spec: Services are isolated and deployed independently using Docker and Kubernetes.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Utilizes CDNs to cache and distribute content geographically closer to users.
data.technical_spec: Employs Open Connect Appliance (OCA) servers to efficiently deliver streams.

data.title: Data Pipeline
data.description: Handles massive amounts of data for streaming analytics and recommendations.
data.technical_spec: Built on Apache Kafka, Hadoop, and Spark to process real-time data streams.

data.title: Global Infrastructure
data.description: Data centers are deployed globally to ensure low latency and high availability.
data.technical_spec: AWS cloud infrastructure is leveraged for scalability and resiliency.

data.title: Backend Resilience
data.description: Emphasizes on failover strategies to reduce downtime in case of server failures.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Hystrix for circuit breaker patterns and resilience4j for fault tolerance.
